revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine}}

The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine}}

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine}}

- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth===
According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds ==
The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including:
- Main hall (本殿)
- Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮)
- Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials"
- Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues)
- Chōzuya (water purification basin)
- Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns)
- Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines===
- {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
- {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance===
Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access ==
The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also ==
- {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō)
- Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.comment: Text replacement - "菅原道真" to "Sugawara no Michizane"
revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).
== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit).== See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also! == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844年|天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3年}}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. === Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.

=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15年}} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.

[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.

=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
