revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] to [[Category:Son-sha]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=draft}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅|12=draft}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=draft}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website <!--Raiden Shrine (Satte)--> <!--Raiden Shrine (Satte)--> <!--Raiden Shrine (Satte)-->

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing imported pages]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=draft}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅|12=draft}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=draft}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website <!--Raiden Shrine (Satte)--> <!--Raiden Shrine (Satte)--> <!--Raiden Shrine (Satte)-->

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|250px]]
拝殿（2012年7月） |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[幸手市]][[中 (幸手市)|中]]四丁目21番10号 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 47.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 6.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = [[別雷命|別雷之命]]
[[倉稲魂命|配祀倉稲魂命]]
[[菅原道真]]『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（190ページ）』幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市[[教育委員会]] [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）[[3月26日]]発行 |社格 = [[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 田宮の雷電様 |例祭 = |神事 = }}{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|4|47.6|N|139|43|6.8|E|region:JP}} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[幸手市]][[中 (幸手市)|中]]四丁目21番10号（旧地名：[[大字]][[幸手宿|幸手]][[小字|字]]田宮）に所在する[[神社]]である。 == 概要 == この[[雷電神社]]は[[別雷命|別雷之命]]・[[倉稲魂命|配祀倉稲魂命]]・[[菅原道真]]の三柱を[[祭神]]とし、古くには田宮の庄幸手領の[[総鎮守]]であったが、[[明治維新|明治維新後]]の[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）4月以後は[[村社]]に指定されている。『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（190ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十三年三月二十六日 発行本殿は[[1844年]]（[[天保]]15年）11月に[[上棟]]の[[改築|再建]]が行われており、[[1856年]]（[[安政]]3年）6月2日にも再建が行われている。
雷電神社の由緒としては、「第11代[[垂仁天皇]]の10年のある日に[[天地]]が[[振動]][[雷鳴]]し、[[水田|水田中]]に身の丈7[[寸]]余りの[[金|黄金]]の御神像が[[天]]から降り、[[村民]]のだれもが驚いた。村民は一社を建立したが、当時周囲には水田が広がるのみで[[住宅|民家]]はなく、わずかに当神社が建つのみであった。ゆえに「水田の中にある社宮」ということから、世の人々はこの地一帯を「田宮」と呼んだ。」というものである。『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（190ページ・249ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十三年三月二十六日 発行また、[[雷神]]は[[水]]との関わりが深いため、特に[[農民]]からの[[信仰]]を集めた。他に社殿の裏手に「[[瘤]]神社」・「[[疣]]権現」・「[[疱瘡]]宮」と彫られた石が建立されており、[[皮膚病]]の人々の信仰の姿を知ることができる。『現地所在の案内板』　幸手市教育委員会 設置
[[境内社]]として[[稲荷社]]、名称不明の一社、[[御嶽神社|御嶽仮山]]が祀られている。境内施設として、[[本殿]]（[[社殿]]）、[[鳥居]]（「正一位 雷電宮」の神額）、案内板、「村社雷電神社」と彫られた[[石碑]]、[[狛犬]][[対|一対]]、[[灯籠]]（複数）、[[手水舎]]、[[力石]]（複数）、境内社（名称不明の境内社に鳥居あり、稲荷社には鳥居なし）がある。 石碑は、「伊勢太々御神楽」、「伊勢参宮記念」、「田宮町 子供神輿新調記念」、「[[二十三夜|二十三夜塔]]」（複数）、「[[奉納]][[畑]]七[[畝 (単位)|畝]]九[[歩 (尺貫法)|歩]]」、「[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦太神]]」（複数）、「稲荷大明神」、「御嶽山」（複数）、「[[三笠山]]」、「寛心行者」、「両部 [[水神宮|江戸水神宮]]」、「瘤神社」、「疣大権現」がある。
その他、仮山（模擬山）、[[オリエンテーリング]]のチェックポイント、田宮自治会館、[[ツバキ]]の[[木]]、[[マキ科|マキ]]の木、[[ケヤキ|欅]]の木、[[イチョウ]]の木、[[モミジ]]の木、[[サツキ]]、[[ヤツデ]]、[[シュロ|棕櫚]]、[[ササ|笹]]などである。『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（171ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十三年三月二十六日 発行『幸手市史 特別版　幸手歴史物語 川と道（57ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十四年一月三十日 発行 == アクセス == * [[File:Tobu Nikko Line (TN) symbol.svg|15px|TN]] [[東武日光線]][[幸手駅]]（東口）より北北東方へ[[徒歩所要時間|徒歩]]にて約10分（道程約500[[メートル|m]]）[http://yahoo.jp/pfSP31 幸手駅-雷電神社周辺] - Yahoo!ロコ ホームページ。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目・周辺 == * 大正新道（[[埼玉県道152号加須幸手線]]） * [[聖福寺 (幸手市)|聖福寺]] * 幸手市立幸手[[中学校]] * [[正福寺 (幸手市)|正福寺]] * 幸手北モール * [[浅間神社 (幸手市北)|浅間神社]] * [[幸手宿]]（[[日光街道]]、[[埼玉県道65号さいたま幸手線]]） * [[幸宮神社 (幸手市)|幸宮神社]] * [[妙観院 (幸手市)|妙観院]] * [[大中落悪水路]] * [[香取神社 (幸手市中)|香取神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html 「伝統行事」幸手の環境を学ぼう] 幸手市役所ホームページ * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html 幸せロードマップ] - 幸手市役所ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:雷電神社|さつて]] [[Category:幸手市の建築物]] [[Category:幸手市の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[菅原道真]]『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（190ページ）』幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市[[教育委員会]] [[2001年]]（[[平成]]13年）[[3月26日]]発行 |社格 = [[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 田宮の雷電様 |例祭 = |神事 = }}{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|4|47.6|N|139|43|6.8|E|region:JP}} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[幸手市]][[中 (幸手市)|中]]四丁目21番10号（旧地名：[[大字]][[幸手宿|幸手]][[小字|字]]田宮）に所在する[[神社]]である。 == 概要 == この[[雷電神社]]は[[別雷命|別雷之命]]・[[倉稲魂命|配祀倉稲魂命]]・[[菅原道真]]の三柱を[[祭神]]とし、古くには田宮の庄幸手領の[[総鎮守]]であったが、[[明治維新|明治維新後]]の[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）4月以後は[[村社]]に指定されている。『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（190ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十三年三月二十六日 発行本殿は[[1844年]]（[[天保]]15年）11月に[[上棟]]の[[改築|再建]]が行われており、[[1856年]]（[[安政]]3年）6月2日にも再建が行われている。
雷電神社の由緒としては、「第11代[[垂仁天皇]]の10年のある日に[[天地]]が[[振動]][[雷鳴]]し、[[水田|水田中]]に身の丈7[[寸]]余りの[[金|黄金]]の御神像が[[天]]から降り、[[村民]]のだれもが驚いた。村民は一社を建立したが、当時周囲には水田が広がるのみで[[住宅|民家]]はなく、わずかに当神社が建つのみであった。ゆえに「水田の中にある社宮」ということから、世の人々はこの地一帯を「田宮」と呼んだ。」というものである。『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（190ページ・249ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十三年三月二十六日 発行また、[[雷神]]は[[水]]との関わりが深いため、特に[[農民]]からの[[信仰]]を集めた。他に社殿の裏手に「[[瘤]]神社」・「[[疣]]権現」・「[[疱瘡]]宮」と彫られた石が建立されており、[[皮膚病]]の人々の信仰の姿を知ることができる。『現地所在の案内板』　幸手市教育委員会 設置
[[境内社]]として[[稲荷社]]、名称不明の一社、[[御嶽神社|御嶽仮山]]が祀られている。境内施設として、[[本殿]]（[[社殿]]）、[[鳥居]]（「正一位 雷電宮」の神額）、案内板、「村社雷電神社」と彫られた[[石碑]]、[[狛犬]][[対|一対]]、[[灯籠]]（複数）、[[手水舎]]、[[力石]]（複数）、境内社（名称不明の境内社に鳥居あり、稲荷社には鳥居なし）がある。 石碑は、「伊勢太々御神楽」、「伊勢参宮記念」、「田宮町 子供神輿新調記念」、「[[二十三夜|二十三夜塔]]」（複数）、「[[奉納]][[畑]]七[[畝 (単位)|畝]]九[[歩 (尺貫法)|歩]]」、「[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦太神]]」（複数）、「稲荷大明神」、「御嶽山」（複数）、「[[三笠山]]」、「寛心行者」、「両部 [[水神宮|江戸水神宮]]」、「瘤神社」、「疣大権現」がある。
その他、仮山（模擬山）、[[オリエンテーリング]]のチェックポイント、田宮自治会館、[[ツバキ]]の[[木]]、[[マキ科|マキ]]の木、[[ケヤキ|欅]]の木、[[イチョウ]]の木、[[モミジ]]の木、[[サツキ]]、[[ヤツデ]]、[[シュロ|棕櫚]]、[[ササ|笹]]などである。『幸手市史調査報告書第10集 村と町 -往時の幸手-（171ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十三年三月二十六日 発行『幸手市史 特別版　幸手歴史物語 川と道（57ページ）』　幸手市史編さん室 編集　幸手市教育委員会 発行　平成十四年一月三十日 発行 == アクセス == * [[File:Tobu Nikko Line (TN) symbol.svg|15px|TN]] [[東武日光線]][[幸手駅]]（東口）より北北東方へ[[徒歩所要時間|徒歩]]にて約10分（道程約500[[メートル|m]]）[http://yahoo.jp/pfSP31 幸手駅-雷電神社周辺] - Yahoo!ロコ ホームページ。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目・周辺 == * 大正新道（[[埼玉県道152号加須幸手線]]） * [[聖福寺 (幸手市)|聖福寺]] * 幸手市立幸手[[中学校]] * [[正福寺 (幸手市)|正福寺]] * 幸手北モール * [[浅間神社 (幸手市北)|浅間神社]] * [[幸手宿]]（[[日光街道]]、[[埼玉県道65号さいたま幸手線]]） * [[幸宮神社 (幸手市)|幸宮神社]] * [[妙観院 (幸手市)|妙観院]] * [[大中落悪水路]] * [[香取神社 (幸手市中)|香取神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html 「伝統行事」幸手の環境を学ぼう] 幸手市役所ホームページ * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html 幸せロードマップ] - 幸手市役所ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:雷電神社|さつて]] [[Category:幸手市の建築物]] [[Category:幸手市の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|ja|幸手駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Station|qq=}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=}}, {{ill|Satte Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Asama Shrine|qq=}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)|version=103068548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}

The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|qid=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qid=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|qid=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qid=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|qid=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qid=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].

The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.

The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|WD=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|qq=|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|qq=|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|WD=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|WD=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|WD=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}

== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|WD=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Satte, Saitama|Satte, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]].
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from [[:en:Satte Station|Satte Station]] (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, [[:en:Sengen shrine|Satte Asama Shrine]], and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (幸手市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Satte Naka Raiden Shrine Haiden 1.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Satte|WD=Q868971}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.07988888888889|N|139.71855555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660222]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|en|Satte, Saitama|WD=Q868971|lt=Satte, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Betsuraijin|ja|別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsuraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Historically, it served as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} of the Tamiya District within Satte, but it has been classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}.
== Overview == The shrine’s main deity is Betsuraijin, along with auxiliary deities Ukanomitama and Sugawara no Michizane. The current main hall (本殿) was reconstructed in {{nihongo|1844 (Tenpō 15)|1844th year |天保15th year }} and underwent another reconstruction in {{nihongo|1856 (Ansei 3)|1856年|安政3rd year }}.
=== Origin Myth=== According to local tradition, during the 10th year of {{nihongo|Emperor Suinin's reign|垂仁天皇}}, a golden divine statue measuring about 7 sun (approx. 21 cm) descended from the heavens into a rice field. The astonished villagers built a shrine to enshrine the deity. The surrounding area, initially a plain of rice fields without residences, came to be known as "Tamiya" (田宮), meaning "shrine in the rice field."
The shrine is also closely associated with agricultural practices due to its connection with thunder, which is essential for rainfall and fertility. It has long been revered by farmers. Additionally, behind the shrine, there are stone monuments labeled "Kobu Shrine" (瘤神社), "Wart Deity" (疣権現), and "Smallpox Shrine" (疱瘡宮), reflecting its role as a site of healing and protection for skin ailments.
== Shrine Grounds == The grounds feature several associated structures and items, including: - Main hall (本殿) - Torii gate with the inscription "Shōichi-i Raiden-gū" (正一位 雷電宮) - Stone monuments commemorating events such as "Ise Taiko Kagura" and "Ise Pilgrimage Memorials" - Komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) - Chōzuya (water purification basin) - Multiple tōrō (stone lanterns) - Sacred trees including camellia, Japanese cedar, zelkova, ginkgo, and maple
=== Sub-Shrines=== - {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} - {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}
=== Cultural Significance=== Stone monuments related to historical events and healing rituals, such as the "23rd Night Tower" (二十三夜塔) and stones dedicated to Sarutahiko Ōkami (猿田彦大神), are present within the precincts.
== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|en|Satte Station|WD=Q7426528|lt=Satte Station}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Satte Kōmyōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Satte Asama Shrine}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
== External Links == * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kankyou/1/sattecityenvironmentnavigation/sattenokankyowomanabou/rekishibunnka/6715.html "Satte’s Traditional Events"] - Satte City Official Website * [https://www.city.satte.lg.jp/sitetop/soshiki/kenkouzoushin/3/1/5229.html "Happiness Roadmap"] - Satte City Official Website
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)|version=103068548|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:雷電神社_(幸手市)]]

== Access == The shrine is approximately a 10-minute walk (500 meters) northeast from {{ill|Satte Station|en|Satte Station|WD=Q7426528|lt=Satte Station|1=Satte Station}} (Tobu Nikko Line East Exit). == See Also == - {{ill|Satte-juku|ja|幸手宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte-juku|1=Sattejuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483989}} (a station on the Nikkō Kaidō) - Nearby landmarks including {{ill|Satte Kōmyōji|ja|幸宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satte Kōmyōji|1=Q11483974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483974}}, {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Satte Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}}, and the Tamiya neighborhood
